title: Test explicit production targets for operator visibility intents

claims.statement: Production currently maintains separate Show-scoped Preview and Program snapshots and Take is the only command that changes Program.
Linux production operators need physical controls because browser context switching creates latency, confusion, timing errors, and visible production mistakes.
claims.evidence: Doctrine-Driven Development Discovery for the Linux operator capability

claims.statement: The discovered work includes content updates, component visibility, and precisely timed Scene changes, from one action through a coordinated workflow.
claims.evidence: Doctrine-Driven Development Discovery for the Linux operator capability

claims.statement: A physical control must distinguish active, inactive, failed, and unknown state; confirmation absent after three seconds cannot be represented as current truth.
claims.evidence: Doctrine-Driven Development Discovery for the Linux operator capability

claims.statement: H-001: Requiring every operator intent to name Preview or Program, with a separate direct-Program authorization, can support immediate live operation without collapsing the two production buses.

Review accepted H-001 only for the in-process production domain after target validation, bus isolation, authorization, idempotency, and source immutability passed; renderer and hardware observation were not inferred.
This Slice does not determine network transport, hardware integration, device identity, action catalogs, coordinated workflow representation, or durable state feedback.

successCriteria.statement: A minimal visibility intent names its Show, component, desired visibility, operation identifier, expected target revision, and Preview or Program target.
successCriteria.verification: Canonical protocol type checks and ProductionService tests.

successCriteria.statement: A missing or invalid target is rejected and a direct Program intent is rejected unless the caller presents the explicit domain capability for Program mutation.
A Preview visibility intent changes and increments only Preview; an authorized Program visibility intent changes and increments only Program.
successCriteria.verification: State-machine tests compare both buses before and after each intent.

successCriteria.statement: Replaying the same operation and payload does not apply it twice, while reusing the operation identifier for another intent is rejected.
The intent returns active or inactive resulting server state, failures are explicit, and the in-process Slice completes within the discovered three-second confirmation budget.
Runtime visibility changes never mutate the saved Scene supplied to Production.
successCriteria.verification: Source-isolation tests compare the original Scene before and after intents.

definitionOfDone.statement: CHG-0010 records SDD-001 as an experiment, distinguishes facts from H-001, and makes no Control API or Stream Deck implementation claim.
Only the canonical visibility-intent contract, the in-process production domain, and focused tests change in this Slice.
Review evaluates whether H-001 preserved explicit targeting, bus isolation, authorization, idempotency, resulting server state, and source immutability without claiming renderer observation.
ADR-0005 was created only after the Slice evidence was reviewed, an overclaim was corrected, and the human authority accepted H-001 within the demonstrated boundary.
Governance, quality, test, and build suites pass for the final Slice manifest.
definitionOfDone.evidence: npm run governance:check and npm run check and npm run build
